tipRules.tip: KVD: 'The right jerkbait cadence varies day to day — some days I wait nearly a minute between twitches, some days only a few seconds. Water temp and weather play a big factor but sometimes it's trial and error. It's all about feel and experience.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Depth is critical in cold water jerkbait fishing — you want the bait slightly above the fish or directly in front of them. They won't chase far when metabolism is low and they certainly don't go down after a bait.'
KVD: 'Color is crucial for jerkbaits — it's a very visual technique and fish get a really good look as the bait sits still between twitches. You need the right blend of visibility and naturalness.'
